topic: truck maintenance — reefer unit making noise

turns.content: okay so my reefer unit is making a noise that i do not like at all and i need help figuring out if this is a go to the shop now situation or a keep driving and pray situation
Let's figure it out. Can you describe the noise -- is it a grinding, a squealing, a rattling, a clicking? And when does it happen -- all the time, only when the compressor kicks on, at certain RPMs?

its like a rattling slash grinding kind of thing and it happens when the compressor cycles on it runs normal for a while and then when it kicks back on there is this noise for about 30 seconds and then it goes away and the unit runs fine and temperatures are holding so far but that noise man it sounds expensive

The fact that temperatures are holding is a good sign -- it means the refrigeration cycle is still working properly. A rattling/grinding on compressor startup that goes away after 30 seconds could be a few things:

1. A loose mounting bolt or bracket vibrating until the compressor gets up to full speed
2. The compressor clutch engaging rough -- could be a worn clutch plate
3. Debris or ice buildup near the condenser fan

How cold is it outside right now? And how long has this been happening?

its about 40 degrees outside im in maryland right now and i first noticed it maybe two days ago when i was loading up in new jersey it was colder up there like mid 30s and thats when it started i thought maybe it was just the cold making things stiff but its still doing it now in maryland where its a little warmer

Two days and getting no better -- that rules out a temporary cold-weather thing. If it were just cold oil or stiff bearings, it would have cleared up by now especially in the warmer temps.

The compressor clutch is my main suspect. When a clutch plate starts wearing, it can rattle or grind on engagement until the plate fully seats and the compressor gets to speed. It's not an emergency yet if temps are holding, but it's the kind of thing that gets worse not better.

Can you see the reefer unit from outside? Any visible damage, loose panels, or anything hanging?

i checked yesterday when i stopped and i didnt see anything obvious no loose panels nothing hanging but i am not a mechanic you know on the old flatbed days the most maintenance i ever did was tighten straps now i got this whole refrigeration machine on the back of my trailer and i barely understand how it works i just know cold good warm bad

Ha, cold good warm bad is honestly the most important thing. You don't need to be a reefer mechanic -- you just need to know when to get it to someone who is. Here's what I'd suggest:

Keep driving for now since your temps are holding. But monitor it closely -- check your temperature readout every hour instead of every few hours. If the noise gets louder, lasts longer than 30 seconds, or your temps start drifting, that's when you need to pull into a shop ASAP.

Do you know where the nearest Thermo King or Carrier dealer is on your route?

i think theres a thermo king place somewhere near baltimore but i would have to check and the thing is i got a delivery tomorrow morning in richmond so i cant really afford to stop for hours at a shop right now because if i miss this delivery window thats a whole problem with dispatch and the receiver and everybody is going to be mad

I understand the pressure. Here's the practical approach: make your Richmond delivery tomorrow morning, then immediately get the reefer looked at before you load your next shipment. There are Thermo King and Carrier service centers all along I-95 in the Richmond area. You could call ahead tonight and schedule a check for tomorrow afternoon.

The risk of driving another 12-16 hours with temps holding is low. The risk of loading another 40K worth of product onto a reefer with a developing compressor issue is much higher.

yeah that makes sense deliver first then get it checked before i take on another load because if this thing dies in the middle of a loaded run thats a disaster and i already have enough stress without worrying about forty thousand dollars of somebody elses food going bad on my watch

Exactly. An empty trailer with a reefer problem is an inconvenience. A loaded trailer with a reefer problem is a catastrophe. Get the delivery done, then make the shop your next stop. They can probably diagnose it in an hour and if it's the clutch, parts are usually available same day for Thermo King units.

alright im going to do that and im going to check the temp every hour tonight like you said and also maybe pray a little bit because bondye bon but also bondye please dont let this compressor die on me tonight in maryland with a full load of frozen dinners i will call a thermo king place in richmond first thing tomorrow and get on their schedule thanks for talking me through this man i was about to panic

No need to panic -- you've got a plan now. Deliver, then shop. Keep an eye on those temps overnight and you'll be fine. If anything changes drastically before Richmond, pull off and call the shop as an emergency. But odds are you'll make it there no problem. Good luck with the run tonight.
